 {"id":"bd-24mj.5","title":"Make search scoring bonuses configurable via presets","description":"Text search scoring in src/analysis/search.rs (lines 186-199) uses hard-coded bonuses:

  title_bonus: 0.3 (if title contains query)
  id_bonus: 0.2 (if ID contains query)
  token_coverage_weight: 0.5
  Final: (token_coverage * 0.5 + title_bonus + id_bonus).min(1.0)

The --search-preset flag already exists with presets like 'impact-first'. These bonuses should be folded into the preset system rather than being separate hard-coded values.

NOTE: This is lower priority because --search-weights already exists as a CLI flag for custom weight overrides, making this partially addressed.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":30,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:44.102005435Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:51:03.835879799Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:51:03.835231692Z","close_reason":"Named constants + rationale docs for search bonuses (commit 2f76d4a)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","configurability","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24mj.5","depends_on_id":"bd-24mj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:06:44.102005435Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-24mj.5","depends_on_id":"bd-24mj.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:01.701335749Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Also e2e_robot_matrix.rs not committed.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:44:33.941802289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-05T06:07:28.791338570Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T06:07:28.791311860Z","close_reason":"All 5 conformance tests pass. Burndown off-by-one fixed, insights key casing fixed, priority field mapping fixed. E2E and stress test files ready to commit.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-2bpl","title":"Legacy capability parity recovery program: replace stale completion claims with verified coverage","description":"Background:
The currently closed parity umbrellas and docs claim a level of completion that we cannot responsibly assert from the present evidence. The codebase is strong and broad, but the project still needs a disciplined capability-parity program that distinguishes between implemented behavior, tested behavior, and merely documented intent.

Goal:
Create the canonical issue tree for getting `bvr` to a truthful state where it can do everything legacy `bv` can do for real operator and agent workflows, while allowing Rust-only improvements and better TUI presentation. Pixel matching is explicitly not the goal; workflow, behavior, and operator outcomes are.

Scope:
This epic covers parity auditing, implementation of newly discovered gaps, proof harness expansion, and documentation truthfulness. Each child should be self-contained enough that a future agent can work from the bead alone without reopening old planning markdown.

Acceptance:
1. The parity claim is backed by evidence instead of optimism.
2. Core legacy workflows are either implemented, proven equivalent, or explicitly de-scoped with rationale.
3. The final tracker tree itself is sufficient as the project plan.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:41.516406660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:51.016860241Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:51.016287677Z","close_reason":"Legacy capability parity recovery program COMPLETE. All tracks closed: audit (track 1), robot/CLI (track 2), TUI (track 3), export/pages/workspace (track 4), docs/verification (track 5). 271 beads closed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["legacy-port","meta","parity"],"comments":[{"id":128,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Program note:
This tree intentionally supersedes the stale "full parity achieved" narrative. Use it to answer a narrower but more honest question: can `bvr` do every job legacy `bv` can do for robot users and terminal operators? If yes, prove it. If not, create or link a concrete fix bead. Visual improvement is allowed; unsupported workflow regressions are not.","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.1","title":"Build the parity baseline and gap ledger from code and real workflows","description":"Background:
Before changing more code, we need a fresh baseline that compares what legacy `bv` actually does against what `bvr` demonstrably does today. The previous parity closure mixed real evidence with forward-looking assertions.

Goal:
Turn the tracker into the new source of truth for parity status. This track should inventory legacy workflows, define evidence standards, and record a concrete gap ledger that downstream implementation tracks can execute against.

Acceptance:
- The tracker contains a capability matrix spanning robot CLI, loader/model semantics, TUI workflows, and export/pages integrations.
- Every gap has a child bead or an explicit de-scope rationale.
- The final matrix distinguishes implemented, proven, partial, divergent-by-design, and unknown states.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:41.632806927Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T03:43:14.783239736Z","closed_at":"2026-03-14T03:43:14.782692389Z","close_reason":"Capability inventory, evidence standard, and fresh gap ledger are now recorded in tracker notes/comments; execution can proceed on robot, TUI, and integration audit tracks","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","parity","planning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:41.632806927Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.1.1","title":"Inventory legacy capability surfaces and map them to concrete bvr modules and workflows","description":"Purpose:
Create the canonical capability inventory that downstream beads rely on. This should enumerate, in tracker form, the legacy surfaces that matter: loader semantics, robot outputs, TUI operator workflows, export/pages flows, workspace aggregation, hooks, preview/watch loops, and path/config resolution behavior.

What to capture:
- The user-visible workflow or operator goal.
- The legacy implementation anchor (docs, code area, or fixture).
- The corresponding Rust modules and tests.
- Whether current status appears proven, partial, unknown, or divergent.

Done means:
Future agents can understand the full scope of parity work from the tracker alone.","notes":"Capability inventory snapshot:

1. Data model and ingest semantics
- Legacy workflow: load issue data correctly from `.beads`, preserve status/dependency semantics, ignore malformed records safely, and resolve project/workspace roots the same way legacy `bv` does.
- Rust module anchors: `src/model.rs`, `src/loader.rs`, `src/error.rs`, `src/cli.rs`, `src/main.rs`.
- Current proof anchors: `tests/cli_model_validation.rs`, `tests/test_parse_timestamp.rs`, conformance fixtures, workspace/history integration tests.
- Provisional status for planning: partially proven. Broad coverage exists, but this family remains high risk because many recent parity bugs were really path/preference/resolution mismatches.

2. Graph analysis and recommendation engine
- Legacy workflow: build the blocking-dependency graph and derive triage/plan/insights/forecast/suggest/history outputs from correct semantics.
- Rust module anchors: `src/analysis/mod.rs`, `graph.rs`, `triage.rs`, `plan.rs`, `forecast.rs`, `alerts.rs`, `suggest.rs`, `diff.rs`, `history.rs`, `git_history.rs`, `label_intel.rs`, `search.rs`, `recipe.rs`, `correlation.rs`, `causal.rs`, `drift.rs`, `whatif.rs`, `advanced.rs`, `brief.rs`, `cache.rs`.
- Current proof anchors: `tests/conformance.rs`, `tests/schema_validation.rs`, `tests/e2e_robot_matrix.rs`, `tests/stress_fixtures.rs`, module-local unit tests.
- Provisional status for planning: mostly proven for core families, but some non-core or recently touched workflow surfaces still need fresh-audit confirmation rather than trusting stale parity prose.

3. Robot/CLI contract surface
- Legacy workflow: agents invoke deterministic `--robot-*` commands and get the right payload shape, defaults, filters, sorting, omission rules, and error behavior.
- Rust module anchors: `src/main.rs`, `src/cli.rs`, `src/robot.rs`, plus all analysis modules above.
- Covered command families seen in repo/docs: triage/next/plan/insights/priority/diff/history/forecast/capacity/burndown/graph/alerts/suggest/search/label-health/label-flow/label-attention/recipes/docs/schema/sprint surfaces/metrics/feedback/drift/correlation-impact/file-intel/workflow briefs.
- Current proof anchors: `tests/conformance.rs`, `tests/schema_validation.rs`, `tests/e2e_robot_matrix.rs`, `tests/robot_alerts.rs`, `tests/robot_burndown_scope.rs`, `tests/robot_history_since.rs`, `tests/admin_cli.rs`, `tests/background_mode.rs`, `tests/cli_model_validation.rs`.
- Provisional status for planning: substantial coverage exists, but audit must verify semantics-by-command rather than trusting the parity ledger.

4. Human TUI workflow surface
- Legacy workflow: users can inspect, search, filter, prioritize, explore dependencies/history, and switch views efficiently in a keyboard-first terminal workflow.
- Rust module anchors: `src/tui.rs`, `src/main.rs`, embedded snapshots under `src/snapshots/`.
- Core modes explicitly present in repo/docs: Main, Board, Insights, Graph, History, plus Rust-only modes Actionable, Attention, Tree, LabelDashboard, FlowMatrix, TimeTravelDiff, Sprint.
- Current proof anchors: lib unit tests in `src/tui.rs`, snapshot files, keyflow journeys, e2e journey snapshots, `README.md` key map, `FEATURE_PARITY.md` TUI sections.
- Provisional status for planning: core functionality is broad, but capability parity still has to be judged at the operator-workflow level rather than by keybinding counts or snapshot existence.

5. Export, pages, preview, watch, and deploy workflows
- Legacy workflow: export artifacts, preview server, watch/live-reload loops, and pages wizard/deploy flows work reliably for operators and generate correct bundle/runtime behavior.
- Rust module anchors: `src/export_md.rs`, `src/export_sqlite.rs`, `src/export_pages.rs`, `src/pages_wizard.rs`, `src/viewer_assets.rs`, `src/main.rs`.
- Current proof anchors: `tests/export_md.rs`, `tests/export_pages.rs`, `tests/e2e_export_pages.rs`, `scripts/e2e_preview_pages.sh`.
- Provisional status for planning: implemented and actively tested, but still a high-value audit target because bundle/runtime and failure-path semantics are easy to overclaim.

6. Workspace, hook, repo-path, and cwd-sensitive integration semantics
- Legacy workflow: running from workspace root, nested repo, alternate cwd, explicit repo path, or explicit beads file still uses the intended project/config/hooks/history roots.
- Rust module anchors: `src/loader.rs`, `src/main.rs`, `src/export_md.rs`, `src/export_pages.rs`, `src/pages_wizard.rs`.
- Current proof anchors: `tests/e2e_workspace_history.rs`, `tests/cli_model_validation.rs`, `tests/export_md.rs`, `tests/export_pages.rs`.
- Provisional status for planning: partially proven and especially important. Recent real bugs came from this family, so the new audit should treat it as a root-cause category rather than a side detail.

7. Operational and release surfaces
- Legacy workflow: admin/agent flows, background mode, profiling/metrics, CI verification, and artifact-oriented debugging are available and understandable.
- Rust module anchors: `src/agents.rs`, `src/main.rs`, `build.rs`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`.
- Current proof anchors: `tests/admin_cli.rs`, `tests/background_mode.rs`, CI workflow, benchmark harness, release/profile docs.
- Provisional status for planning: mixed. Some surfaces are Rust-specific and should be documented as additive, not confused with strict legacy parity.

Planning implication:
The inventory supports the current track split: baseline/audit, robot CLI, TUI workflows, integration/export/workspace, and final evidence/docs. The highest-risk cross-cutting theme is path/root-resolution correctness; the highest-risk truthfulness theme is stale documentation that treats broad implementation as proven equivalence.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.590899400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T03:42:00.560888919Z","closed_at":"2026-03-14T03:42:00.560392860Z","close_reason":"Capability inventory now recorded in tracker notes/comments with module anchors, proof anchors, and provisional status across all major legacy workflow families","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.590899400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":134,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Inventory recorded:
I mapped the current project into seven capability families with module anchors, proof anchors, and provisional planning status: data ingest/model semantics; graph analysis engine; robot/CLI contract surface; human TUI workflows; export/pages/preview/watch/deploy workflows; workspace/hook/repo-path/cwd-sensitive integration semantics; and operational/release surfaces. The inventory explicitly flags path/root-resolution as the highest-risk cross-cutting category and warns that the current docs overstate proof in several areas.","created_at":"2026-03-14T03:42:00Z"}]}
